steps.title: Spot the key signal
steps.content: The passage says the people of the time "may have thought of the paintings as something akin to art" but "it is entirely possible that they did not—we simply cannot say for certain." The blank fills how the original understanding of the paintings is described. Since we cannot know, the blank must mean not known.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean not known or unable to be determined. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — unknown: means not known — directly matches "we simply cannot say for certain" about how the paintings were understood. ✓
  • Choice B — unalterable: means unable to be changed — describes permanence, not uncertainty; the problem isn’t that the meaning is fixed but that it can’t be determined. ✗
  • Choice C — unsurprising: means expected or predictable — implies we know what to expect, contradicting the passage’s statement that we cannot say for certain. ✗
  • Choice D — uncontroversial: means agreed upon without dispute — the passage’s uncertainty implies debate is possible, not that there is settled agreement. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
